summary: exxpress-utils postinstall exfiltrates host and filesystem data over HTTP
details: The package name is a character-insertion typosquat of the popular 'express' package. On install, postinstall.js collects host identity data via os.hostname() and os.userInfo() (line 215), enumerates filesystem paths via fs.existsSync() checks (lines 91-103), and transmits the collected data over HTTP (require('http') at line 256). The combination of host/user enumeration, filesystem reconnaissance, and outbound HTTP in a postinstall lifecycle script is the canonical installer-harm shape: any developer running `npm install exxpress-utils` has their machine fingerprinted and file-presence signals shipped off-host without consent. The typosquat name further indicates intent — unsuspecting installers who mistyped `express` are the target population.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
